DevRel tools and platforms, including GitHub, Stack Overflow, and Discord, and how to leverage them for community engagement

knowledge.what_it_is: A hook is a function in React that allows you to 'hook into' React state and lifecycle methods from functional components, enabling them to manage their own state without needing to use class components.
what_you_will_learn: useState — managing local component state with an array of values
useEffect — handling side effects like API calls, timers, or DOM mutations after render
useContext — consuming React Context without prop drilling through intermediate components
useReducer — managing complex state logic with a reducer pattern similar to Redux
useMemo — memoizing expensive computed values to avoid unnecessary recalculation on re-render
useCallback — memoizing callback functions to prevent unnecessary child component re-renders
useRef — accessing DOM nodes directly and persisting mutable values across renders without triggering re-render
Custom hooks — extracting reusable stateful logic into standalone functions (e.g., useLocalStorage, useFetch)
Error handling with useEffect — catching and handling errors in side effects
